definitions.technical: A stablecoin structured so that yield generated from underlying reserve assets (typically U.S. Treasury investments) passes through to the token holder rather than being retained by the issuer. This pass-through structure causes classification as a security under U.S. law.
definitions.lay: A type of stablecoin where the interest earned from the reserves is paid to the people holding the tokens, similar to a savings account. Because holders earn returns, these tokens are legally treated as investment securities, not as simple payment tools.
definitions.parenthetical: (a stablecoin passing reserve yield through to holders, classified as a security and excluded from the GENIUS Act's payment stablecoin framework)
definitions.legal_significance: Yield-bearing stablecoins are explicitly excluded from the GENIUS Act's payment stablecoin framework because their pass-through yield structure triggers securities classification under the Howey test. They fall under SEC jurisdiction rather than OCC oversight.
